post-install-output: * Monolog capture is automatic — every channel is wired for you.
Disable it with <comment>log_ui.capture_monolog: false</comment> (e.g. in production).
* In production the UI is fail-closed: set <comment>LOGUI_PASSWORD</comment>, or use <comment>log_ui.access: delegate</comment>
and protect the route in security.yaml.

contents: # Access control. In dev/test the UI is open; in prod it is fail-closed.
#   access: password  → log in with the LOGUI_PASSWORD generated in .env, sent as the
#                       (Put a real secret in .env.local for production.)
#   access: delegate  → LogUI gates nothing; protect ^/_logui yourself in security.yaml
#                       (recommended in prod — works with your normal login + navigation).
# Everything below is optional — these are the defaults, shown for discoverability.
# capture_monolog: true                  # auto-wire the handler onto every Monolog channel
